channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a series of executive orders aimed at boosting the struggling coal industry, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on a lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m. following a three-drug injection at Florida State Prison for the April 2000 strangling of Janet Acosta.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Waiver allows Trump administration to bypass environmental rules to build more border walls
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — A waiver issued Tuesday by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security allows the federal government to bypass environmental regulations and begin construction to add more barriers along the U.S-Mexico border in Southern California even as illegal crossings have plummeted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Parents arrested, charged after third grader brings gun to a elementary school
item.description: Both parents were charged with recklessly leaving a loaded, unsecured firearm in a manner that endangered the life or safety of a child under 14.
item.dc:creator: Kendal McAuley, NewsNation

item.title: Rapper 'Fat Flow' gets 33 years for kidnapping migrants
item.description: A judge has sentenced a rapper calling himself Fat Flow to 33 years and four months in prison for the 2023 kidnapping of seven migrants in Juarez, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: An immigration judge in Louisiana says she will decide later this week whether the government can continue to detain Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia University activist facing deportation for his role in pro-Palestinian campus protests.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline And Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former New Mexico college football player faces possible life in prison after drug trafficking conviction
item.description: A former college football player could be sentenced to life in prison for smuggling meth into a New Mexico jail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: A teenage boy described by his family as autistic, nonverbal and intellectually disabled remains in critical condition after being shot in his backyard by Idaho police last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Claudia Lauer And Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Massachusetts teacher charged with assaulting nonverbal student
item.description: The incident was witnessed by another staff member who police said immediately reported it to the school’s principal.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Nuggets fire head coach, GM with three games remaining before the playoffs
item.description: The decision comes as the Nuggets only have three regular-season games remaining before the NBA playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: NASA astronaut arrives at International Space Station aboard Russian spacecraft
item.description: NASA astronaut Jonny Kim and Russian cosmonauts Sergey Ryzhikov and Alexey Zubritsky entered the ISS after a successful launch from Kazakhstan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House insists Trump won't budge on 104 percent China tariff
item.description: The White House on Tuesday insisted that President Trump won’t back down on his threat to impose a more than 100 percent tariff rate on China, significantly escalating the trade war between the two nations hours before the deadline approaches.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Veterinarian goes missing after video showed him kicking horse
item.description: A Las Vegas veterinarian is missing following a viral video that shows him kicking a horse in the head. On Thursday, Dr. Shawn Frehner was paid to administer anesthetic shots for some horses at a Nevada property.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KTLA 5

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Legal status revoked for 985,000 migrants who entered US under Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is terminating parole protections for people who entered the country through the CBP One app, revoking legal status for nearly a million migrants who came to the U.S. during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Fraternity brothers help reunite missing dog with owners after nearly 5 years
item.description: A dog missing for five years had a sweet reunion with its owner thanks to the help of a fraternity at The University of Southern Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More Americans call taxes unfair: Poll
item.description: More Americans say that the amount they will pay in federal income taxes this year is "unfair" than those who say their amount is reasonable, according to a new poll, and most say that corporations and wealthy people are not paying enough.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Las Vegas hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery
item.description: Kimberly LeMarbre said she was woken up in the operating room and told the surgery to address her second diagnosis of thyroid cancer could not be performed in January of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, NewsNation

item.title: Last chance to claim $1,400 stimulus check from 2021 nears
item.description: Taxpayers who haven’t filed their 2021 tax returns may still be eligible to receive one (or more) stimulus payments sent during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: Parents charged after gun goes off in 3rd grade student's backpack at Spotsylvania County school
item.description: The parents of a third grader who had a gun discharge in their backpack at Lee Hill Elementary School are now facing charges, deputies said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees, which was said to have been planted by Jackson in the 19th century, was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US expected a big travel year, but overseas visitors
item.description: Olja Ivanic looked forward to welcoming some cousins from Sweden to her Denver home in June. Ivanic and the four travelers were planning to go hiking in Colorado and then visit Los Angeles and San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, KRON4

item.title: Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre leaves hospital after crash with school bus
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre has left an Australian hospital a week after she was involved in a crash with a loaded school bus that made international headlines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump assassination attempt suspect Ryan Routh sought rocket launcher from Ukraine: docs
item.description: Prosecutors revealed that Ryan Routh, the alleged would-be assassin of President Trump, tried to obtain military-grade arms from a contact in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats targeting 35 Republicans in 2026
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: Energy Department considers more than 40 percent of its staff non-essential as layoffs loom
item.description: The Energy Department's portfolio includes both energy technology and innovation as well as nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9
